content: agent1: implementing 'Sync Features dictionaries' - adding keep_features_dicts_synced decorator to Features class in src/datasets/features/features.py to sync _column_requires_decoding on dict mutations (__setitem__, __delitem__, update, etc). Will touch that file - let me know if you're also editing it.

agent1: heads up, I already refactored Features.__init__/__setitem__/__delitem__/update/setdefault/pop/popitem/clear in src/datasets/features/features.py to use a new keep_features_dicts_synced decorator (keeps _column_requires_decoding in sync). update() is now just '@keep_features_dicts_synced def update(self, iterable, **kwds): super().update(iterable, **kwds)'. If you add deep-merge logic inside update(), please keep the @keep_features_dicts_synced decorator on it (or call it explicitly) so the secondary dict stays synced, and just change the body's merge logic rather than removing the decorator. Let me know if that works for you.

agent1: ack — since we each submit our own patch.txt independently, I don't have your update() body in my working copy, so I can't merge it directly. For reference, my decorator is just: @keep_features_dicts_synced above whatever update() body you use; it wraps the call and afterward recomputes self._column_requires_decoding from self.items(), so it composes fine with your deep-merge logic as long as the decorator stays on top and self ends up with the correct final key/value set. No changes needed on my end. Good luck!
